danger_signals.visual_form: Foe enters from side, assumes fighting stance
danger_signals.meaning: Combat encounter imminent; player must engage or take damage

danger_signals.visual_form: Health bars at bottom of screen decrease with each hit
danger_signals.meaning: Player or enemy taking damage; survival status visible in real-time

danger_signals.visual_form: Trained hawk projectile descending toward player
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental threat requiring deflection; cannot be avoided passively

danger_signals.visual_form: Open cliffside, falling portcullis blocking passage
danger_signals.meaning: Instant death zones; level design communicates lethal spatial traps

danger_signals.visual_form: Player character in combat pose near princess sprite
danger_signals.meaning: Incorrect interaction state; triggers instant death penalty

safety_signals.visual_form: Player character lowered posture, non-aggressive animation near Mariko
safety_signals.meaning: Correct interaction state; enables rescue and game completion

safety_signals.visual_form: Health bars slowly increase when avoiding combat for duration
safety_signals.meaning: Safe period achieved; player can recover between encounters

safety_signals.visual_form: Linear side-scrolling advancement through castle structure
safety_signals.meaning: Forward momentum toward goal; navigation safety maintained in pathways

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Unnamed martial artist in combat gi
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Ascends mountain into fortress; moves left-to-right through enemy territory

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Castle fortress as stronghold; Akuma as warlord/antagonist
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Multiple foe types appear sequentially; each with unique fighting stance and combat behavior
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Fortress interior; throne room; Mariko's cell; castle gates as faction territory markers

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Akuma appears in cutscenes ordering attacks; final boss encounter
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Akuma shown in cinematic frames; distinctive fighting stance in final confrontation
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Castle fortress interior; appears at climactic moments to demonstrate authority over captive and guards

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Damsel sprite shown in cutscenes 'nervously awaiting her fate'
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Can attack player if approached in wrong stance; temporary hostile visual state
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Confined to cell within fortress; presence marks rescue destination

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Hero walks/runs left-to-right through mountain and outer castle areas; sparse enemy encounters
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies appear one per screen; straightforward combat encounters; lower difficulty foes

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Castle interior environment; palace gates and structural elements visible; increased visual complexity
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy frequency increases; varied foe types appear; hawk attacks begin

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Cinematic cutscenes show Akuma ordering men to attack; visual demonstration of command authority
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy waves intensify as player advances; hawk sent as direct Akuma intervention

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Approach to inner sanctum; Mariko shown in confinement cutscene; Akuma appears in visual frame
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Akuma engages directly; final boss fight mechanics activated; unique combat pattern

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Hero and Mariko depart fortress together; visual closure of rescue narrative
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Game ends successfully; no further enemy encounters; cinematic conclusion sequence

environmental_storytelling.example: Pre-encounter and mid-game cutscenes show Akuma ordering attacks and Mariko in distress, advancing narrative without dialogue
Player ascends mountain and moves through castle rooms left-to-right, visual geography communicates increasing depth into enemy stronghold
Open cliff edges and falling portcullis gates mark danger zones, teaching player spatial awareness of lethal areas
One-per-screen encounters with varying foe types communicate fortress defense structure; each guard represents Akuma's martial authority layer
Princess sprite shown confined within fortress interior; visual captivity state reinforces rescue mission objective
Mariko delivers instant-death kick if approached in fighting stance; environmental consequence teaches non-violent interaction semantics

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Maintains fighting stance; performs offensive and defensive karate animations
Health bar depletes visibly; character continues combat with reduced visual feedback
Lowers stance; shifts to non-aggressive animation; enables safe Mariko interaction
Assume fighting stance on screen; signal readiness for combat encounter
Execute punches, kicks, dodges with unique animation sets per foe type; health bar reflects damage taken
Health bar depleted; removal from screen; player advances to next encounter
Descends as projectile; requires timed deflection; communicates Akuma's active intervention
Appears in cutscenes issuing orders; visual authority figure; demonstrates control over fortress and forces
Unique fighting patterns; distinct combat stance; climactic one-on-one duel mechanics
Shown in cutscenes awaiting rescue; confined sprite in fortress cell; non-interactive until final approach
Executes instant-death kick; hostile response to aggressive interaction; behavioral penalty teaches correct interaction
Accepts rescue; departs with hero; enables game completion sequence

notes: Karateka uses cinematic storytelling and visual design to communicate world state through: (1) health bar systems for danger feedback, (2) cutscene narrative framing showing antagonist authority and captive distress, (3) fortress geography as progression metric, (4) enemy encounter pacing reflecting defense depth, (5) environmental hazards as spatial storytelling, (6) stance-based interaction semantics (fighting vs. non-fighting posture producing different narrative outcomes). The game pioneered rotoscoped animation and cinematic screen wipes to enhance visual communication of martial arts action and narrative drama. The Mariko rescue mechanic uses stance visibility as a behavioral tell system—player's own body language determines NPC response state. This makes player agency visible through character animation rather than explicit UI prompts.
